items.title: 4-day office return for Global Affairs Canada workers delayed over lack of space
items.summary_auto: Global Affairs Canada is postponing the requirement for its unionized employees to be on site four days a week due to a lack of office space, Radio-Canada has learned.

items.title: Carney 'disappointed' in Canada's ambassador in Washington for English-only invite to MPs
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney said he is disappointed that Canada's ambassador to the U.S. issued an English-only invitation to MPs for an upcoming reception in Washington.

items.title: In tearful speech, N.L. tourism minister decries 'bullying' following AI controversy
items.summary_auto: Newfoundland and Labrador Tourism Minister Andrea Barbour opened Thursday’s House of Assembly session with a tearful speech about bullying she says she’s faced since taking office.

items.title: CBC president denies broadcaster is biased or poaches journalists from smaller markets
items.summary_auto: At a parliamentary committee hearing on journalism on Thursday, CBC president Marie-Philippe Bouchard denied the public broadcaster's news coverage is biased or that it poaches journalists from smaller media outlets in regional markets.

items.title: Israeli strikes, fire kill 5 in Gaza and West Bank, medics say
items.summary_auto: Israeli airstrikes killed at least four Palestinians in Gaza on Thursday, and Israeli soldiers shot and killed a 15-year-old during an army raid in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, Palestinian health officials said.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Best red carpet looks from The Devil Wears Prada 2
items.summary_auto: The cast of The Devil Wears Prada reunited on the red carpet for the long-awaited sequel to the 2006 hit.

items.title: Sidney MacEwen resigns from P.E.I. cabinet amid conflict over lobster fishing licence
items.summary_auto: Prince Edward Island cabinet minister Sidney MacEwen has resigned from his role on executive council, the province said in a news release Thursday afternoon.

items.title: Prime minister should be required to divest assets, says committee
items.summary_auto: Mark Carney and other Canadian prime ministers should be required to divest their investment portfolios when they assume office, not just put them in a blind trust, the House of Commons ethics committee recommends in a new report.

items.title: Kuwaiti court acquits detained U.S. Kuwaiti journalist of all charges, lawyers say
items.summary_auto: A Kuwaiti American journalist has been acquitted in Kuwait of all charges after nearly two months in detention during a crackdown on press freedom and social media posts on military activity in the Middle East amid Iran's war with the U.S. and Israel.

items.title: Why are veggies so expensive? Consumers crunched by climbing cucumber costs
Warner Bros. shareholders approve $81-billion US takeover by Paramount
items.summary_auto: An overwhelming majority of Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders voted in support of selling the business to Paramount on Thursday. The approval by shareholders is a big step toward the deal being finalized, which would have big implications for the media landscape.

items.title: How the PWHL is trying to discourage teams from tanking
B.C. sees 'no reason' to put U.S. booze back on liquor store shelves, minister says
items.summary_auto: British Columbia doesn't intend to put U.S. products back on B.C. Liquor Store shelves until Canada's trade and tariff war with the United States has been resolved, the province's minister for jobs and economic growth says.

items.title: Taylor Swift, Bad Bunny, Drake top Spotify’s most streamed artists list
items.summary_auto: Taylor Swift has topped Spotify's first list of its most streamed artists of all time, published Thursday morning.

items.title: Northern and central Manitoba hit by ice, fierce winds amid blizzard, freezing rain warnings
items.summary_auto: A snowfall warning that was in place for most of northern Manitoba has intensified into a blizzard warning for the entire region.

items.title: WestJet increases fees for checked baggage, matching recent Air Canada move
items.summary_auto: WestJet is increasing the price many passengers will pay to check baggage less than two weeks after a similar Air Canada move, with increases of $5, $10 and $50 to certain checked luggage fees.

items.title: Killing of Calgary-born scientist tied to mysterious U.S. deaths, disappearances despite quick arrest
items.summary_auto: The shooting death of a Calgary-born astrophysicist in mid-February in front of his Southern California residence sent shockwaves through his academic and professional circles.

items.title: Smoke blankets Montreal as recycling plant fire prompts health warning, investigation
items.summary_auto: A fire at a recycling plant in Montréal-Est prompted an investigation by Quebec's Environment Ministry, a health warning from Santé Montréal and caused a plume of thick, white smoke to drape the city in acrid-smelling fumes.

items.title: Rate of Innu child protection removals has plummeted, but investigations continue to climb
items.summary_auto: Innu children are still overrepresented in the child protection system, though far less likely to be removed from their homes than they were nearly a decade ago.

items.title: 30 writers from across Canada make 2026 CBC Short Story Prize longlist
items.summary_auto: The winner will receive $6,000, a writing residency and have their work published on CBC Books.

items.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats choking Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: President Donald Trump said Thursday he has ordered the U.S. military to "shoot and kill" small Iranian boats that deploy mines to choke traffic through the Strait of Hormuz as Israel says its ready to resume attacks on Iran.

items.title: Ford's controversial omnibus budget bill passes final vote at Queen's Park
items.summary_auto: The Ford government’s omnibus budget bill, along with changes to freedom of information laws, passed its final vote at Queen’s Park on Thursday. Bill 97 skipped committee sessions and instead was discussed in a heated question period Thursday and debates in a rare late-night sitting Wednesday.

items.title: Bodies of knowledge: U of A medical school thanks 'remarkable' donors and their families
items.summary_auto: Before she died at age 96, Doreen Edna Buhler lived a difficult but remarkable life. But death was not the end of Buhler's story. As part of her final wishes, she donated her remains to the University of Alberta's Anatomical Gifts Program to further a future generation of medical professionals.

items.title: 'New face, same dictatorship,' says Madagascar's Gen Z activist following protester arrests
items.summary_auto: Youth-led protests drove out Madagascar's president in October 2025. It was supposed to be a revolution leading to systemic change, but activists say the new regime is even worse.

items.title: Spike in shipping costs to Nunavut via Churchill, Man., is just business, operator says
items.summary_auto: The Nunavut government’s exclusive maritime carrier is bumping up shipping costs for the upcoming sealift season — particularly through Churchill, Man., to Kivalliq communities. It now costs exactly the same amount of money as shipping goods from Bécancour, Que.

items.title: This 19-year-old attempted suicide. Hospital staff sent him to a homeless shelter
2 trains collide in Denmark, leaving 4 people critically injured
items.summary_auto: Two local trains collided head-on in Denmark on Thursday, injuring at least 17 people, four of whom were in a critical condition, the emergency service told Reuters.

items.title: Why airlines are cutting flights as jet fuel prices surge
items.summary_auto: Airlines around the world are cutting flights as they face what some experts are calling the worst aviation crisis ever, with fuel shortages caused by the blockage of the critical Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Tickets for Punjabi pop concerts are selling fast — especially in Canada
items.summary_auto: Two of the biggest global names in Punjabi music are set to take over Canada’s largest venues —  underscoring the genre’s explosive rise worldwide. Diljit Dosanjh and Karan Aujla are launching Canadian tours drawing demand promoters say rivals some of the biggest names in hip-hop.

items.title: Why Ontario's temporary HST rebate on new builds may not lead to hot housing market deals
items.summary_auto: Because the budget has yet to pass, specific guidance on the rebate is not yet available. To get an understanding of what we do know about it, CBC News spoke with real estate experts on how it could work and what it means for buyers.

items.title: Square dances and cell signal dead zones: Rangers confront Canada’s Arctic reality
items.summary_auto: A 52-day, 5,200-kilometre Canadian Ranger patrol along the Northwest Passage revealed the human and technological limits of Arctic operations. From impromptu square dances in remote communities to frozen batteries and fragile communications gear, the Canadian military is finding the North demands adaptation and partnership.

items.title: Americans are paying more attention to Canada. Should we worry?
items.summary_auto: American media, politicians and other right-wing influencers are paying more attention than ever before to Canada's domestic politics. Why does the MAGAsphere suddenly care so much, and what does it mean for Canada?

items.title: Amid year-long state of emergency, Neskantaga First Nation calls for health-care intervention
items.summary_auto: Neskantaga First Nation has been under a state of emergency since last April after its only nursing station flooded. Local leaders say access to health care hasn’t improved in the remote Ojibway community, which has also been under a boil-water advisory for 31 years, and community members face ongoing challenges accessing timely medical transportation for appointments in other areas.

items.title: Autopsy report released in death of teen girl singer D4vd is charged with killing
items.summary_auto: Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the 14-year-old girl singer D4vd is charged with killing, died from penetrating wounds to her upper body, according to an autopsy report released Wednesday.

items.title: U.S. navy secretary out of his job in latest Pentagon shakeup
items.summary_auto: U.S. Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his job, the Pentagon abruptly announced Wednesday, the first head of a military service to depart during President Donald Trump's second term but just the latest top defence leader to step down or be ousted.

items.title: How Japan is rebuilding its war machine | About That
items.summary_auto: Japan lifted a ban on lethal weapons exports that had been in place since the Second World War. Andrew Chang explains how the country is repositioning itself on the global defence market, and why it is rethinking its military readiness in a major shift from pacifist politics.

items.title: Former Nike executive to take over as Lululemon's new CEO
items.summary_auto: The Vancouver-based athleisure retailer announced Wednesday that Heidi O’Neill will become its next chief executive officer on Sept. 8, when she will also join its board.

items.title: Diljit Dosanjh pays tribute to Komagata Maru passengers ahead of sold out Vancouver show
items.summary_auto: Punjabi superstar launches his Aura World Tour in front of 55,000 fans at B.C. Place on Thursday.

items.title: Ford rejects idea of putting U.S. booze back on shelves unless White House concedes trade war
items.summary_auto: Ontario Premier Doug Ford is rejecting any idea of putting U.S. alcohol back on store shelves in Ontario without the White House first conceding in the ongoing trade war.

items.title: Investigation into Tumbler Ridge mass shooting has entered final stages: B.C. premier
items.summary_auto: Premier David Eby offered the update during a meeting with Tumbler Ridge Mayor Darryl Krakowka, almost three months after Jesse Van Rootselaar killed eight people, including six schoolchildren, then herself.
